page.title: Middlewares
page.lead: Middlewares are functions used for cross-cutting concerns. Middleware functions let you run code before route handlers, enabling concerns such as authentication, logging, request preprocessing, and more.

basic.title: Basic middlewares
basic.description: To create a middleware, define an arrow function following this pattern:

order.title: Middleware order
order.description: Middlewares are executed in the same order as they are registered:

scope.title: Global vs Group vs Route middlewares
scope.description: Middlewares can be registered globally, per route group, or for a specific route:

combined.title: Combining middlewares
combined.description: You can stack multiple middlewares in a route.

tip: Always call the <mark class="docs-highlight">next()</mark> function to continue the request flow. If you forget it, requests will be blocked.
